3.happening: Move the cursor to the next slot we will turn into a running total.
3.why: We sweep left to right so each slot can reuse the total already built to its left.
3.invariant: Every slot before the cursor already holds a running sum; index 0 was correct from the start.

4.happening: Add the running total on the left ({nums[i-1]}) into this slot ({nums[i]} → {nums[i] + nums[i-1]}).
4.why: nums[i-1] already sums everything up to i-1, so one addition extends the total to include nums[i].
4.invariant: After this line, nums[i] holds the sum of nums[0..i].

5.happening: Every slot has been rewritten into a running sum — return the finished array.
5.why: The array was transformed in place, so no second array was ever allocated (O(1) extra space).
5.invariant: nums[k] equals sum(nums[0..k]) for every index k.

loop.happening: Sweeping across the array one slot at a time.
loop.why: Each new slot builds directly on the total already computed to its left.
loop.invariant: Slots left of the cursor are finished running sums.

update.happening: Folding the previous running total into the current slot.
update.why: A single addition turns nums[i] into the sum of everything up to i.
update.invariant: The cursor's slot becomes a running sum this step.

return.happening: The whole array now reads as a running sum.
return.why: Computed in one pass, in place — O(n) time, O(1) extra space.
